sections.summary: This section defines key terms related to data storage and processing services, including data storage devices, providers, and sectors. It specifies that data storage or processing services must be commercial, involve computers, or be explicitly listed in the rules. The definitions clarify the scope of entities and services covered under the Act.

requirements.text: data storage device means a thing (for example, a disk or file server) containing (whether temporarily or permanently), or designed to contain (whether temporarily or permanently), data for use by a computer
data storage or processing provider means an entity that provides a data storage or processing service
data storage or processing sector means the sector of the Australian economy that involves providing data storage or processing services

data storage or processing service means: (a) a service that enables end-users to store or back-up data and is provided on a commercial basis; (b) a data processing service that involves the use of one or more computers and is provided on a commercial basis; or (c) a service specified in the rules

sections.heading: Compilation
sections.title: Definition of unauthorised access, modification or impairment
sections.summary: This section defines what constitutes unauthorised access, modification, or impairment of computer data or programs. It specifies that such actions are unauthorised if the person is not entitled to cause them, with examples including employees exceeding their authority as employees or agents. It also states that whether the person can be identified is immaterial for determining unauthorised actions.

requirements.text: Access to computer data or computer program is unauthorised if the person is not entitled to cause that access
Modification of computer data or computer program is unauthorised if the person is not entitled to cause that modification
Impairment of electronic communication to or from a computer is unauthorised if the person is not entitled to cause that impairment
Impairment of availability, reliability, security or operation of computer data or computer program is unauthorised if the person is not entitled to cause that impairment
A person who is an employee or agent of the responsible entity would exceed their authority as such an employee or agent in causing access, modification or impairment in relation to the asset
It is immaterial whether the person can be identified for determining unauthorised actions

sections.source_quote: 74 12N Meaning of unauthorised access, modification or impairment (1) For the purposes of this Act: (a) access to: (i) computer data; or (ii) a computer program; or (b) modification of: (i) computer data; or (ii) a computer program; or (c) the impairment of electronic communication to or from a computer; or (d) the impairment of the availability, reliability, security or operation of: (ii) computer data; or (iii) a computer program; by a person is unauthorised if the person is not entitled to cause that access, modification or impairment. (1A) The following is an example of a situation where a person is not entitled to cause access, modification or impairment of a kind mentioned in subsection (1): a person who is an employee or agent of the responsible entity for an asset would exceed the person's authority as such an employee or agent in causing such access, modification or impairment in relation to the asset. (2) For the purposes of subsection (1), it is immaterial whether the person can be identified.
sections.relevance_reason: Defines unauthorised data actions critical for security compliance

sections.summary: A critical infrastructure risk management program must be a written program that applies to a responsible entity for critical infrastructure assets. The program may relate to specific assets and must comply with rules specifying background checks under the AusCheck scheme.

The program must mitigate the relevant impact of such a hazard on the asset where reasonably practicable
The program may relate to one or more specified critical infrastructure assets

sections.summary: A cyber security exercise must be conducted by an entity responsible for a system of national significance and must test the entity's response capabilities to cyber security incidents. sections.summary: This section establishes conditions under which the Minister may authorise the Secretary to issue directions for critical infrastructure incidents. The authorisation requires specific conditions to be met before it can be granted.
sections.dtia_summary: This section defines when and how the Minister can authorise emergency directions for critical infrastructure incidents, impacting data transfer and processing during cyber incidents. It requires proportionality and technical feasibility checks for any data sharing or actions.

sections.heading: Compilation
sections.title: Compensation for property acquisition and electronic service rules
sections.summary: The Commonwealth must compensate entities if property acquisition under the Constitution occurs on unjust terms. Disputes over compensation may be resolved in Federal Court or State Supreme Court. Electronic transactions rules under the Electronic Transactions Act 1999 do not apply to notices, directions, or instruments under this Act. An independent review of the Act's operation must be conducted by the Minister within 12 months after commencement.

sections.source_quote: If the operation of this Act would result in an acquisition of property from an entity otherwise than on just terms, the Commonwealth is liable to pay a reasonable amount of compensation to the entity.

sections.heading: Compilation
sections.title: Independent review and rule-making authority
sections.summary: The Minister must conduct an independent review of the Act's operation within 12 months after commencement, with the report tabled in Parliament within 15 sitting days. The Parliamentary Joint Committee on Intelligence and Security may review the Act's operation and effectiveness for up to 5 years after the Security Legislation Amendment (Critical Infrastructure) Act 2021 receives Royal Assent. The Minister may make rules via legislative instruments for matters required or permitted by the Act or necessary for its implementation.
